target.title: feat(browser): My Browser node-anchored turn routing (stacks on #93411)
target.body:
> **Stacked on #93411 — that must merge first.** Builds on the run-scoped `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive from #93411; the diff currently overlaps #93411's commits and will be rebased to the extension-only delta once #93411 lands. Draft until then.

## What this is for

**The headline use case: fill the long, annoying forms & questionnaires for me.** You're logged into some portal — a multi-page application, an expense claim, a detailed survey — and you'd rather not hand-type 30 fields. My Browser keeps you in your *own* Chrome (your session, your cookies, your tabs) and lets you tell the side-panel agent, in plain English, what you want; it fills the form in your real browser, **self-checks its work**, and stops short of submitting so you review first. The same loop draws on natural language, the agent's OpenClaw **memory** (your saved details), and **web lookup** for fields it needs to recall or research.

*Proven end-to-end* — one instruction ("fill this pizza order: Cameron Beeley, +44 7700 900142, …, large, bacon + extra cheese, 19:00, comment 'ring the doorbell twice', don't submit") → the agent navigated, filled **every field type** (text, radio, checkboxes, time, textarea), **snapshotted to verify its own work** ("Let me take a screenshot to verify the text fields too"), and left it unsubmitted for review:

![Form-fill demo — from one natural-language instruction the agent fills a multi-field form (text, radio, checkboxes, time, textarea) in the user's real browser, self-verifies via snapshots, and leaves it unsubmitted](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-formfill.png)

Everything below is the plumbing + hardening that makes this safe to run in your real browser. See **User-story traceability** (after the findings) for how each fix protects this use case.

## Summary

Makes "My Browser" a usable side-panel copilot on top of node-anchored tool-scoping. Four blocks:

**1. Node-anchored turn routing (the original core).** Routes the side-panel turn **through the hosting node** as an authenticated node-originated `agent.request`, so the gateway confines that turn's tools via `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411), and pins the browser proxy back to that same node. Falls back to a direct gateway turn when no node hosts the bridge.
- Node-host event emitter (`node-event-emitter.ts`, re-exported via `plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime`); extension bridge forwards the side-panel `agent.request` via an `onAgentRequest` callback threaded from `server-lifecycle`; browser tool pins to the turn's `hostingNodeId`.

**2. Browser-copilot persona as a system prompt.** `server-lifecycle` injects `BROWSER_COPILOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` as `extraSystemPrompt` on every node-originated side-panel `agent.request` (server-side, not client-injectable). Without it the agent tends to refuse browser tasks / fetch over HTTP; with it, it acts through the browser tool, defers only CAPTCHA/2FA/payment, and won't reload a page it's already on.

**3. Side-panel UX (`sidepanel.js`/`background.js`).**
- **Per-tab agent sessions** — deterministic key per pinned tab (`…:thread:tab-<id>`), so each attached tab has its own resumable conversation; "+ New session" resets the thread.
- **Live tab-context injection** — each turn carries the pinned tab's current URL+title, so the agent knows where it is without a tool round-trip and won't re-navigate a page it's on.
- **Streaming render fixes** — render the gateway's authoritative full text (no duplicate/garbled deltas), render line breaks, and split inter-tool commentary into separate persisted bubbles with a `→ <tool>` step line per tool call (the panel opts into `tool-events`).

**4. Bridge reliability.**
- **Page-only target announce** — only real `page` targets are announced to Playwright; worker/`blob:`/service-worker/cross-origin-iframe child targets are dropped (they were announced as phantom pages, hanging the next snapshot ~47s on heavy pages like signup flows).
- **Per-turn CDP keepalive** — reuse the cached CDP connection within an idle TTL, force a fresh reconnect after a gap; skip the per-op throwaway health-probe when a live connection is cached.
- **Idempotent `attachTab` + `getTargetId`** — re-attaching an already-connected tab no longer crashes Playwright with a Duplicate-target error; the panel can resolve a tab's CDP targetId to pin turns to it.

Out of scope: the `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive itself (#93411). The node-bridge driver uses its own literal `extension-bridge`, distinct from the legacy `extension` → `existing-session` value (whose doctor migration is preserved), so there is no deprecation collision.

## Linked context

Depends on #93411 (gateway-core `byNode`). Related #93347 (closed — superseded by the authenticated-node approach).

## Review findings addressed (local ClawSweeper / Codex review, head 11b72146a8)

Local + public ClawSweeper / Codex review rounds flagged the items below; all are resolved or explained:

1. **[HIGH] Unauthenticated extension bridge** (`extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **fixed.** When `gateway.auth.token` is configured, the `/extension` relay handshake must present a matching HMAC on `?token=` (recomputed server-side via WebCrypto, mirroring the extension's `deriveRelayToken`) or the socket is rejected (close 1008); tokenless loopback stays trusted-local. Proven live on the deployed build — `no-token → 1008`, `wrong-token → 1008`, `correct-HMAC → accepted` — plus 4 unit tests (`extension-bridge-server.auth.test.ts`).
2. **[MED] Hard-coded `DEFAULT_TOKEN`** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed.** Removed `test-token-for-container`; tokenless loopback now omits auth instead of sending a placeholder credential.
3. **[high] node-event emission on the public SDK** (`plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime.ts`) — **fixed** (`04d6d08b3b`). `emitNodeGatewayEvent` (and the install primitives) are removed from the public plugin-SDK barrel and moved to a dedicated **private** entrypoint `node-gateway-events-internal` (mirroring `ssrf-runtime-internal`): built for the bundled first-party bridge but excluded from public package exports, so a third-party plugin cannot install an emitter or forge node-attributed events. The extension package-boundary checks (`no-plugin-sdk-internal`, `no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`, `subpaths-exported`) all pass. (The private-subpath form as first implemented broke **runtime** resolution — found via live deploy, fixed in `b3e840c785`; see #14.)
4. **[P1] macOS toggle writes invalid config** (`ConfigStore.swift`) — **fixed.** Added `browser.priorDefaultProfile` to the config schema so the My Browser toggle's state write validates against the strict `browser` object.
5. **[P1] `driver:"extension"` collided with the legacy migration** — **fixed** (`677c875a8e`). The branch had repurposed the legacy `driver:"extension"` literal (which the doctor migrates to `existing-session`, Chrome MCP attach) for the new node-bridge mode *and* deleted that migration, so the doctor would silently rewrite a new-mode profile and legacy upgrades broke. Fixed by giving the new mode its own literal **`extension-bridge`** (zod union + refine, public type, the input-side resolver, the macOS toggle) and **restoring** the deleted `extension → existing-session` profile migration. `doctor-legacy-config` migration test passes; build clean. (The rename initially left the relay gate on the dead `extension` literal — found via live deploy, fixed in `f4f56efa2e`; see #15.)
6. **[P1] Dependency-graph change** (`pnpm-lock.yaml`) — **stacked-base drift; rebase/approval-gated.** The bundled `chrome-extension/package.json` is `private` and adds **no runtime dependencies**; the only lockfile changes are upstream override bumps (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `hono`, `aws`, `acp`) carried by the #93411-stacked base, not this PR. They disappear on the rebase to the extension-only delta. Full resolution needs that rebase or maintainer dependency-guard approval — neither available pre-#93411.
7. **[P2] `config.example.json` relayPort** — **fixed** (`debaa45e3e`). Was `18790`; corrected to `18792` (`DEFAULT_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_PORT`) so the bundled example dials the right bridge port.
8. **[P2] relay URL throws under tokenless auth** (`background-utils.js`) — **fixed** (`e1d56a8aba`). `buildRelayWsUrl` threw on a blank gateway token, but `gateway.auth.mode:"none"` is valid; it now returns a tokenless `/extension` URL (the node bridge accepts it only when it also has no configured token, so a token-protected bridge still rejects it).
9. **[P1] Schema-invalid macOS profile** (`ConfigStore.swift:178`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). The My Browser toggle wrote `{driver:"extension-bridge"}` but the zod profile schema requires `color`; the toggle now writes a `color` so the saved config validates. (Distinct from #4, which added `priorDefaultProfile`.)
10. **[P1] Copilot prompt dropped** (`server-node-events.ts`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). `server-lifecycle` emits `extraSystemPrompt` on the node-originated `agent.request`, but the receiver's `AgentDeepLink` parser omitted the field, so the browser-copilot persona never reached the agent. Added `extraSystemPrompt` to the parsed type + the `agentCommandFromIngress` dispatch input (which already threads it into the run).
11. **[P1] Explicit relay host pointed at the gateway** (`background.js getRelayHost`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). `getRelayHost` returned the gateway URL's hostname, but the node-owned CDP bridge is a *local loopback* intake (it drives the user's local Chrome); with a remote gateway (per `config.example`'s `wss://…ts.net`) the relay would dial the wrong host. It now defaults to `127.0.0.1`, with an explicit `relayHost` override for advanced setups.
12. **[high] Node-routing fail-open** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). A failed node turn silently fell back to a direct (unconfined) `sessions.send`, dropping `gateway.tools.byNode` confinement. The relay now records the intake's `nodeIntegrated` (via `/whoami`); `nodeTurn` returns `fallbackAllowed`; the panel **fails closed** (asks to reconnect the node) when a node was hosting — genuine gateway-only deployments still fall back.
13. **[P2] Options page blocked tokenless loopback** (`options.js`) — **fixed** (`c99fd6ab78`). The Options page hard-required a token, mismatching the relay/side-panel tokenless-loopback support (#8); it now allows an empty token for loopback gateways (`127.0.0.1`/`localhost`/`::1`) while still requiring one for remote gateways.
14. **[P0] F3 seam broke runtime module resolution — found via live deploy** — **fixed** (`b3e840c785`). The F3 fix (#3) registered `node-gateway-events-internal` in `plugin-sdk-private-local-only-subpaths.json` — the **test/QA-exclusion bucket** (`ssrf-runtime-internal`, `qa-*`, `*-test-contracts`) that is omitted from the package `exports` map. In any **installed** package the bridge's runtime `import` threw `ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED`, so `ensureExtensionRelayForProfiles` never bound the relay (the error was swallowed by its own `try/catch`) and node-anchored My Browser silently failed. Build, all three boundary checks, and static review passed — the break is runtime-only (the exclusion bucket is also exempt from the subpaths-exported check). Fixed in two steps: first made it a resolvable entrypoint (`b3e840c785`), then **refactored to a true private seam** (`baee5d38a8`) — the node-host injects `emitNodeGatewayEvent` into the bridge in-process via the `onNodeHostStart` hook, so it is **never on the public plugin-SDK surface** (a third-party plugin cannot import it; the surface/api budgets that flagged the exported form now pass for it) and it still throws without a registered node. `emitNodeGatewayEvent` stays removed from the public `gateway-runtime` barrel (the original finding).
15. **[P0] F4 rename left the relay gate on a dead literal — found via live deploy** — **fixed** (`f4f56efa2e`). `ResolvedBrowserConfig.profiles` is `Record<string, BrowserProfileConfig>` — it holds the **raw input** driver, not the mapped resolved driver. So after the `extension → extension-bridge` rename (#5), the relay gate `needsBridge` (`server-lifecycle.ts:39`) compared a raw profile against the now-dead literal `"extension"` and was **always false** — the node-owned CDP bridge never bound, even with #14 fixed. Gate on the input `"extension-bridge"`. (`config.ts:552` and `profile-capabilities.ts:27` are correct — they operate on the mapped/resolved driver.) Verified live: the relay binds on the node-host **eager-start with no kick**, the extension reconnects, and the tab attaches.
16. **[high] Node-turn status failure could bypass confinement** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`12b9e03fa1`). The `catch` around the `nodeTurn` message treated a *rejected* `chrome.runtime.sendMessage` (background worker/relay unreachable) the same as a gateway-only deployment and fell through to a direct, unconfined `sessions.send`. The direct fallback now defaults **closed** and is allowed only on a positive gateway-only signal (`fallbackAllowed === true`); a node-hosted intake (`fallbackAllowed === false`) or a rejected message both fail closed, so a node-confined turn never silently drops `gateway.tools.byNode`.
17. **[high] node identity not surfaced on the bridge `/whoami`** (`server-lifecycle.ts`) — **fixed** (`1ce3dccc87`). `ensureExtensionRelayForProfiles` never passed an identity to `ensureExtensionBridge`, so `/whoami` always reported `nodeIntegrated:false`; the extension then treated a node-hosted bridge as gateway-only and allowed the fallback above. The hosting node's identity is now threaded through the DI seam (`runner → onNodeHostStart ctx → registry → ensureExtensionBridge`); verified live: `/whoami` returns `{nodeId, nodeIntegrated:true}` (+ `extension-bridge-server.whoami.test.ts`).
18. **[P1] browser CLI status didn't surface the `extension-bridge` driver** — **fixed** (`c03c0c3585`, refined `fc4fac3a29`). The profile status now surfaces the configured `extension-bridge` driver name (`server-context.ts` reverse-maps the resolved `extension` 1:1; `ProfileStatus.driver` type updated). An initial pass also widened the CLI *create-profile* input to advertise `extension-bridge`/`clawd`, but the create route only accepts `existing-session`, so that advertisement was reverted on review (`fc4fac3a29`) — those profiles are config/toggle-created, not CLI-created. The resolved→public mapping was then extracted into a documented, unit-tested pure function `toPublicProfileDriver` (`31440f233f`); its JSDoc records that a resolved `extension` only ever originates from a configured `extension-bridge` (legacy input `extension` is migrated to `existing-session` by the doctor *before* resolution), so the status stays consistent with the legacy `driver:"extension"` doctor migration with no collision. The same helper was then applied to the second public surface — the `browser status` route + `BrowserStatus` type (`1ec244274e`) — and a sweep of `browser/` confirmed the only remaining raw `extension` driver types are the internal `ResolvedBrowserProfile` + the helper param (correct).
19. **[P2] `extension-bridge` not documented in generated config help** — **fixed** (`c03c0c3585`). The zod `driver` union had no `.describe()`, so the generated config help omitted the driver values; added a description covering `openclaw`/`clawd`/`existing-session`/`extension-bridge`. (Build + config-help-quality test + browser-tool tests green.)
20. **[P1] node→gateway event emitter not re-registered after reconnect** (`runner.ts`) — **fixed** (`fc4fac3a29`). `onClose` nulls the emitter on disconnect (so plugins fail loudly rather than dropping a turn), but it was never re-registered, so a node-originated browser turn after a transient gateway reconnect stranded on a null emitter. Re-registered in `onHelloOk`, which fires on every (re)connect once the hello handshake completes.
21. **[medium] unauthenticated loopback CDP bridge face** (`extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **hardened** (`b3533a10c5`). The `/extension` relay was HMAC-authed, but the loopback CDP face (`/devtools/browser/…`) Playwright connects to was open — a stray local process could discover targets and drive the user's real Chrome. The bridge now registers its token in the bridge-auth registry and requires `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on the `/devtools/browser/` upgrade (constant-time `crypto.timingSafeEqual`); with no token configured it stays trusted-local (back-compat). `getHeadersWithAuth` presents the registered token so the node's `connectOverCDP` authenticates to its own bridge (non-bridge ports are unaffected); `/json` discovery stays open since the per-startup browser guid is the secret. Covered by `extension-bridge-server.cdp-auth.test.ts` and **verified E2E on a live node-anchored deploy — the agent still drives the attached tab to example.org with the CDP face now authenticated (no regression):**

![CDP-face auth E2E — the LLM agent drives the attached tab to example.org ("Done — the tab is now on https://www.example.org/") with the loopback CDP face now requiring the bridge token](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/cdp-auth-e2e.png)
22. **[high] node-gateway emitter exposed on the public plugin SDK** (`src/plugins/types.ts`) — **fixed** (`0913003ee2`, finalized `efa5cbd4c4`). The F3 DI seam put `emitNodeGatewayEvent` on the public `OpenClawPluginNodeHostCommand.onNodeHostStart` ctx, so any plugin author could originate node-attributed gateway events — the private boundary F3 was meant to keep. The emitter is removed from the public `onNodeHostStart` ctx type (which now carries only `nodeId`); the node-host passes it on the **runtime** ctx **only to the bundled node-anchored browser bridge**, gated on the **registry plugin identity** (`entry.pluginId === "browser"`, the bundled plugin's manifest id — not a forgeable self-declared cap; `9a82c1d790`) — every other plugin's startup hook gets just `nodeId`; the emitter stays off the public `{nodeId?}` ctx type (the browser plugin reads it via an internal cast). (Three interim passes — a host-internal `plugin-sdk` subpath, an ungated runtime ctx, then a cap-gated ctx — were superseded to drop a package self-resolution, stop the emitter being visible to all plugin hooks, and gate on identity rather than a declarable cap.) Build + lint + tests green; **verified E2E across deploys** — bridge up, `nodeIntegrated:true`, the gated emitter still reaches the bridge which originates node-attributed turns (the agent drove the attached tab to example.net → example.com → iana.org).
23. **[P2] stale byNode tests (run-scoped contract)** — **fixed** (`b58b000d71`). The byNode tests (from the stacked #93411 base) targeted the retired session-global registry (`setSessionHostingNodeId`) instead of the run-scoped `hostingNodeId`, so they failed against the final implementation: byNode is enforced in `createOpenClawCodingTools` (`agent-tools.ts:932`, `resolveNodeScopedToolPolicy(options.hostingNodeId)`) where `hostingNodeId` is threaded through the run (`attempt.ts → params → agent-tools`); `resolveGatewayScopedTools` is a byNode no-op for non-node MCP/HTTP callers. The embedded test now passes `hostingNodeId` (where byNode bites); the gateway-resolver test is rewritten to a single no-op assertion. Both pass (68/68 + green). *(These are #93411 stacked-base test files — fixed here per request; they reconcile on the #93411 rebase.)*
24. **[medium] broad Chrome extension control surface** (`extensions/chrome-extension/manifest.json`) — **audited; manifest reduced to 10 genuinely-used permissions.** History: an initial narrowing (`4a75987af5`) wrongly removed `declarativeNetRequest` + `clipboardWrite` on a **faulty grep** (escaped `\|` matched a literal pipe → false 0 uses), reverted in `5effcef169`. On proper re-audit: `clipboardWrite` is used (`sidepanel.js:696` writeText) and stays; `declarativeNetRequest` backed **only the dead frame-strip rule**, removed in `3b2374b616` (see finding 26). The remaining 10 are all exercised — `debugger` (CDP, 13), `tabs` (22), `storage` (23), `webNavigation.onCompleted`, `sidePanel` (6), `tabGroups` (3), `alarms` (3), `scripting`, `activeTab`, `clipboardWrite`. Host scope required (bridge WebSockets → loopback/`*.ts.net`; `webNavigation` needs any-URL visibility). No over-broad/unused permission remains; the residual concern is product/security acceptance of CDP-based browser control, not a code change.
25. **[high] emitter gate could be shadowed by plugin id** (`src/node-host/plugin-node-host.ts`) — **fixed** (`5effcef169`). Gating the privileged node→gateway emitter on `entry.pluginId === "browser"` alone let a **config-loaded plugin shadow the bundled `"browser"` id** and receive it. Now gated on **both** the registry plugin's trusted bundled origin **and** id: `entry.origin === "bundled" && entry.pluginId === "browser"` — a config/external plugin is `origin !== "bundled"`, so it is **fail-safe denied**. Threaded the plugin record's trust `origin` (`PluginOrigin`) onto the `PluginNodeHostCommandRegistration` entry (type + the single `registry.ts` push site); the field is optional so non-registry fixtures default to `undefined` (denied). `tsgo:core` clean; `loader` (160) + `plugin-node-host` (2) tests pass; oxlint 0/0. **Re-deployed + E2E re-proven on the hardened gate:** the bundled browser plugin (`origin:"bundled"`) still passes — `node.invoke 2520ms` + `live session turn` 9.9s, agent drove the attached tab to iana.org:

![Emitter origin-gate E2E — after hardening the gate to require origin:"bundled" AND id:"browser", the bundled browser bridge still receives the emitter and the agent drives the attached tab to iana.org (a config plugin shadowing the id would be denied)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-gate-e2e.png)
26. **[high] frame-strip rule strips CSP/X-Frame-Options for no consumer** (`extensions/chrome-extension/background.js`) — **fixed** (`3b2374b616`). The `declarativeNetRequest` dynamic rule removed `X-Frame-Options` + `Content-Security-Policy` response headers for the gateway hostname in `sub_frame`s, per its comment *"so the gateway Control UI loads in the side panel iframe"* — but there is **no `<iframe>` anywhere** in the extension (`sidepanel.html`/`options.html` have none). Dead code that needlessly weakened header security. Removed the rule (`updateFrameStrippingRule` + its `gatewayUrl`-change listener + startup call + `FRAME_STRIP_RULE_ID`) and the now-unused `declarativeNetRequest` permission — eliminating the header-stripping entirely. `getGatewayUrl` stays (used elsewhere); extension oxlint delta 0. **Re-deployed + E2E re-proven:** 10 permissions, no DNR, agent drove the tab to example.org (`node.invoke 225ms` + `live session turn` 7.5s):

![Frame-strip-removal E2E — after deleting the dead frame-strip DNR rule and the declarativeNetRequest permission (10 permissions, no CSP/X-Frame-Options stripping), the agent still drives the attached tab to example.org](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-no-framestrip-e2e.png)
27. **[high] bridge auth used the raw (unresolved) gateway token** (`extensions/browser/src/browser/server-lifecycle.ts`) — **fixed** (`55bf3af73f`). The CDP bridge passed the **raw** `getRuntimeConfig().gateway.auth.token` to the relay/CDP auth gate, so a SecretRef (`${SECRET:…}`) or env-provided gateway token would be HMAC'd as an unresolved literal — or missed entirely — breaking valid SecretRef/env-token setups and calibrating the gate to the wrong value. Now resolves the **effective** token via `ensureGatewayStartupAuth` (materializes SecretRefs + env-aware), the same derivation the gateway authenticates against and the control relay (`control-auth.ts`) already uses. `tsgo:all` clean (0 errors). **E2E re-proven:** the extension still handshakes with the resolved token (Connected) and drives the attached tab to wikipedia.org:

![Bridge auth resolution E2E — after resolving the effective (SecretRef/env-aware) gateway token instead of the raw config literal, the extension still handshakes (Connected) and the agent drives the tab to wikipedia.org](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-auth-e2e.png)
28. **[P2] document/privatize the public `onNodeHostStart` hook** (`src/plugins/types.ts`) — **fixed** (`82f91a8757`). Made it a *documented + tested* plugin-API decision (rather than privatizing): the doc now states `onNodeHostStart` is a deliberate public API, but the privileged node→gateway emitter is **intentionally not on its ctx** (only `nodeId`) — it is delivered out-of-band to the bundled browser bridge gated on trusted bundled origin + identity. Added a unit test: the bundled browser plugin (`origin:"bundled"`, id `"browser"`) receives the emitter; a config-loaded plugin **shadowing** the `"browser"` id with `origin:"external"` does **not** — which also locks the finding-25 origin-gate. 3/3 tests pass; `tsgo:core` clean.
29. **[high] bridge auth ignored password-mode shared secrets** (`extensions/browser/src/browser/server-lifecycle.ts`) — **fixed** (`a4df31cb39`). The finding-27 fix resolved the effective token but used only `.auth.token`, so a **password/trusted-proxy-mode** gateway (whose active shared secret is the *password*) was left auth-less at the relay/CDP gate. Now selects the **mode-active** secret (token for token/none, password for password/trusted-proxy), mirroring `resolveBrowserControlAuth`. Clean `tsgo:all` (0 errors); the bot-specified suites pass (`extension-bridge-server.auth`/`.cdp-auth`, `gateway/auth`, `gateway/startup-auth`).
30. **[P1] side panel sent only the token in the gateway handshake** (`extensions/chrome-extension/sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`04b4d10b55`). The client-side complement to findings 27/29: the extension's connect handshake sent `auth: {token}` only, so a **password-mode** gateway rejected the side-panel connection. The gateway accepts `token || password` as the shared secret but the extension can't know the mode, so it now sends the configured credential under **both** fields (the gateway validates only the mode-active one); token mode is unaffected (gateway reads `token` first). Options label updated (token *or* password). **E2E re-proven:** token-mode side panel still connects (Connected) + drives the tab.
31. **[P2] `onNodeHostStart` needed public SDK docs** (`docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`) — **fixed** (`04b4d10b55`). The hook was documented only in a type comment (finding 28). Added it to the public plugin SDK docs (`api.runtime.nodes`): the optional node-host startup hook for long-lived node-anchored services, and that the privileged node→gateway emitter is deliberately off its public ctx (gated to the bundled browser bridge by trusted origin + identity).
32. **[high] unauthenticated `/json` discovery leaked tab metadata** (`extensions/browser/src/browser/extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **fixed** (`960917da3c`). The bridge's `/json/version` + `/json/list` routes returned the CDP debugger URL and **every attached tab's title/URL** with no auth — readable cross-origin (Private Network Access). The CDP-face auth (`b3533a10c5`) gated only the `/devtools/browser` WS upgrade; `/json` discovery stayed open. Now all `/json*` routes are gated with the same bridge token (`isAuthorizedPwUpgrade`): **401** unauthenticated, **200** with the bearer token, open on a tokenless trusted-local bridge (the node's Playwright presents the token via `getHeadersWithAuth`). Added the bot-requested auth tests (unauthorized `/json/version`+`/json/list`→401, authorized→200, tokenless→200; unique port per case to dodge undici keep-alive reuse). Clean `tsgo:all`; **6/6** cdp-auth tests pass; E2E confirms the node still connects + drives the attached tab through the gated route (no regression).
33. **[high] background relay handshake also omitted the password** (`extensions/chrome-extension/background.js`) + **[P2] labeler coverage** — **fixed** (`c8ff4ba153`). The *third* instance of the password-mode bug (after the bridge in 27/29 and the side panel in 30): the background service-worker relay handshake (`background.js:667`) sent only `auth: {token}`, so password-mode gateways rejected the background bridge relay. Now sends `{token, password}` (token mode unaffected — gateway reads `token` first). **Grepped all credential-send sites to close it out:** the only other sends are `buildDeviceBlock` (signs the configured secret = the active secret, valid either mode) and the browser plugin's bridge/control auth (already carries both fields) — so these two extension handshakes were the only `{token}`-only gaps. For the `[P2]`: added a `plugin: browser` block to `.github/labeler.yml` (`extensions/browser`, `extensions/chrome-extension`, `docs/plugins/copilot.md`) so the new surface gets review-routing/ownership labels. E2E re-proven: token-mode background relay still connects + drives the tab.
34. **[medium] bridge CORS was wildcard — `/whoami` host/node identity readable by any website** (`extensions/browser/src/browser/extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **fixed** (`e01d567602`). The bridge set `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` + `Access-Control-Allow-Private-Network` for all routes, so any website could read `/whoami`'s hostname + nodeId over Private Network Access. Now the Origin is reflected **only for `chrome-extension://` origins** (the bundled extension's service worker + side panel); arbitrary http(s) websites get no CORS header (the browser blocks the cross-origin read), and the node's Playwright/curl send no Origin so are unaffected. Added a CORS test (chrome-extension origin reflected, https website → `null`). Clean `tsgo:all`; 7/7 cdp-auth tests pass; **live-verified** on the rig: website Origin gets no `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`, chrome-extension Origin reflected. *(This round also surfaced a test-rig bug: the deploy's `pkill -f openclaw.mjs` missed a leftover bridge process, so earlier runtime spot-checks hit stale code — the committed/built dist was always correct; a broader kill fixed the rig.)*

## User-story traceability

How the hardening above protects the headline use case (and the supporting stories):

| User story | Protected by |
|---|---|
| Fill forms in **my own** Chrome session (cookies/logins/tabs) | node-anchored turn routing (Summary block 1); `driver:"extension-bridge"` drives the *attached* tab over the node-local CDP bridge |
| Each tab is its **own isolated session** | per-tab session keys (block 3); proven by the MELON multi-tab isolation test |
| A **stray local process can't hijack my browser** *or read my tabs* | CDP-face auth (`b3533a10c5`) + effective/active-shared-secret bridge auth (findings **27 + 29**) + gated `/json` discovery (finding **32**) — the loopback CDP endpoint *and* its tab-metadata discovery require my gateway's active secret |
| Only the **bundled bridge** can originate node-attributed turns as me | emitter origin+id gate (finding **25**); `onNodeHostStart` keeps the emitter off the public ctx (finding **28**) |
| The agent only gets the **tools the node allows** | run-scoped `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411 base; stale tests fixed in finding **23**) |
| **No silent submit; no over-broad surface** | the agent stops short of submitting (demo above); manifest pared to 10 *used* permissions + dead CSP-stripping rule removed (findings **24 + 26**) |

**Disposition of the remaining (non-code) review items** — all investigated; actioned where a code action exists:
- **Rebase / "clean rebased diff after #93411"** — genuinely blocked, not deferred-by-choice: prerequisite **#93411 is still OPEN**, so its `byNode` primitive is not in `main`. Rebasing onto `main` now would *delete* the primitive #93680 depends on. Actionable only once #93411 merges.
- **Dependency Guard / "remove the dependency graph change"** — **#93680 adds zero npm dependencies.** `extensions/chrome-extension/package.json` declares no `dependencies`/`devDependencies` (only a static-asset build config), so its entire lockfile footprint is one empty importer entry (`extensions/chrome-extension: {}`). The version churn the guard reports is the stacked-base / branch-divergence delta, which resolves on the post-#93411 rebase — there is no #93680-introduced dependency to remove.
- **Config/default surfaces upgrade behavior** — rechecked, all additive/opt-in with no existing-install impact: `browser.priorDefaultProfile` is `z.string().optional()` (undefined on existing installs); `driver:"extension-bridge"` is a *new* schema literal with the default unchanged at `openclaw` and no legacy config carrying it (so no doctor migration is needed); `gateway.tools.byNode` is additive (inert unless a node is configured).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** a usable node-anchored browser copilot — node-confined turns, a persona that actually drives the browser, per-tab sessions, and a bridge that survives heavy real-world pages.
- **Real environment tested:** an OpenClaw gateway built from this branch driving the bundled extension in a headless Chromium (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) container, plus a device-paired loopback node for the byNode path, plus the standalone bridge + browser-tool unit suites.
- **Evidence after fix (live):**
  - `pnpm build` clean (`BUILD_EXIT=0`).
  - **byNode narrowing** a real node turn: `[byNode] allow=[web_search,image] before=5{tts,web_search,web_fetch,image,canvas} after=2{web_search,image}` (run-scoped via threaded `hostingNodeId`).
  - **Heavy-page (page-only filter):** navigating to `account.proton.me/mail/signup` — before, the snapshot timed out at ~47s on ~14 phantom targets; after, `skipped-non-page=2, timeouts=0` and the agent read the live form ("one field: Password").
  - **Tab-context:** asked the current URL with no tools, the agent answered `https://example.org/ — "Example Domain"` from injected context with `0` navigations (no reload).
  - **Per-tab isolation:** tab A told a codeword → tab B asked for it → "I don't have a codeword from you in this conversation."
  - **CDP keepalive:** per-op connect churn dropped 7→2 across a multi-step turn.
  - **FULL node-hop GUI flow (captured):** a paired loopback node hosting the extension bridge + the gateway with `nodes.browser.node`/`byNode`. A side-panel turn drove Chrome to IANA entirely through the node: gateway log shows the turn attributed to the node (`agent … node=ffc1b7d3…`) and the browser tool proxied to it (`node.invoke ✓` ×3 → `browser.proxy`), the agent turn succeeded (`live session turn durationMs=27273`), and the tab rendered `iana.org` ("Internet Assigned Numbers Authority"). Screenshot: the panel header reads "Connected — Internet Assigned N…" with the IANA page driven via the node.
  - **Relay auth gate (F1) — proven live on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** with `gateway.auth.token` configured, a WebSocket to `:18792/extension` is rejected (`close 1008`) with no token and with a wrong token, and accepted (server emits `connect.challenge`) only with the correct `deriveRelayToken` HMAC. Backed by 4 unit tests.
  - **Bootstrap-deadlock fix — re-proven on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** after killing the node-host (`:18792` → unbound) and restarting it with **no manual relay kick**, the node binds `:18792` at startup and the bridge answers `/json/version`.

**Verbatim run logs (latest build, redacted):**

```
# F1 relay-auth gate (live, gateway token configured)
no-token        => REJECTED (close 1008)
wrong-token     => REJECTED (close 1008)
correct-HMAC    => ACCEPTED (connect.challenge)

# Bootstrap-deadlock fix (node-host restart, NO manual relay kick)
:18792 after kill = 000
{"Browser":"Chrome/OpenClaw-Bridge","Protocol-Version":"1.3","webSocketDebuggerUrl":"ws://127.0.0.1:18792/devtools/browser/<id>"}

# Node-hop turn (side-panel -> node -> attached Chrome tab)
[gateway] agent ... node=ffc1b7d3...        # turn attributed to the hosting node
node.invoke OK x3 (browser.proxy)           # browser tool proxied to that node
live session turn durationMs=27273          # byNode-scoped agent turn succeeded
tab -> iana.org ("Internet Assigned Numbers Authority")

# F3 host-internal seam (extension package-boundary checks)
no-plugin-sdk-internal       : No extension plugin-sdk boundary violations found.
no-src-outside-plugin-sdk    : No extension plugin-sdk boundary violations found.
subpaths-exported            : OK: all referenced openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath> imports are exported.
```

**Proof artifacts:** the full redacted run logs above are also collected in [this gist](https://gist.github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/17fba6011e351775e97d8b21a0d67ab7). Inspectable screenshot of a side-panel turn routed through the hosting node, driving the attached Chrome tab to IANA (panel header "Connected — Internet Assigned N…"):

![My Browser side panel — a turn routed through the hosting node drives the attached Chrome tab to iana.org](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/node_e2e2.png)

**Latest build (after the F3+F4 P0 fixes) — the LLM agent drives the user's real attached tab end-to-end:** on a clean install the relay binds on the node-host eager-start (no kick), the bundled extension reconnects and attaches the live tab, and side-panel turns are handled by the agent itself — routed through the hosting node (`agent … node=ffc1b7d3…`, `live session turn durationMs=7211`), the agent calls its browser tool on the attached tab and replies in the panel. Below: a multi-turn session navigates the live tab `wikipedia.org → github.com` from side-panel turns ("Go to wikipedia.org" → "Now take me to github.com"), each confirmed by the agent (the panel shows "You're now on github.com.").

![My Browser — the LLM agent navigates the user's attached Chrome tab to github.com from a side-panel turn, routed through the hosting node](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-agent.gif)

**Comprehensive re-verification (recorded on `1ce3dccc87`, after the node-identity fix; the only later commits (`12b9e03fa1` fail-closed hardening, `c03c0c3585` driver surfacing, `fc4fac3a29` reconnect emitter re-registration + create-input revert, `31440f233f` driver-mapping extract/test, `1ec244274e` BrowserStatus mapping, `b3533a10c5` CDP-face auth, `0913003ee2`/`efa5cbd4c4`/`fe25b016f8`/`9a82c1d790` emitter off public SDK, identity-gated to the browser bridge; `b58b000d71` byNode test repair; `4a75987af5`→`5effcef169` manifest re-audit + emitter origin-gate, E2E re-proven on iana.org through the hardened gate; `3b2374b616` dead frame-strip rule + DNR removed, E2E re-proven on example.org; `55bf3af73f` effective-gateway-token bridge auth, E2E re-proven on wikipedia.org; `82f91a8757` onNodeHostStart doc + emitter-gate test; `a4df31cb39` active-shared-secret bridge auth + form-fill value E2E; `04b4d10b55` side-panel password-mode handshake + onNodeHostStart SDK docs, handshake E2E re-proven; `960917da3c` gated /json CDP discovery + auth tests, E2E re-proven on iana.org; `c8ff4ba153` background relay password-mode handshake + browser labeler, E2E re-proven on example.org; `e01d567602` bridge CORS restricted to chrome-extension origins, live-verified) do not change these successful-turn behaviors):** `/whoami` now reports `{nodeId, nodeIntegrated:true}` on the node-hosted bridge, so the D-fix fail-closed actually fires.

**🎥 Live video on the latest-head runtime (after the full 12-round review chain, identity-gated emitter + run-scoped byNode + CDP-face auth all live):** the LLM agent drives the attached Chrome tab from a side-panel turn — typed prompt → `→ browser` → the tab navigates live → *"Done — now on https://www.wikipedia.org/"*:

![Live video — the LLM agent drives the attached Chrome tab to wikipedia.org from a side-panel turn, on the latest-head runtime (node-routed, identity-gated emitter)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-latest-head.gif)

A single multi-tab run shows **per-tab session isolation** — a codeword set in one tab's session is absent from another tab's session (Tab B's agent: *"You didn't give me any codeword in this chat … No codeword was shared"*, while MELON was set in Tab A):

![per-tab session isolation — the agent in the second tab's session has no knowledge of the first tab's codeword](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/per-tab-isolation.png)

![motion — codeword set in Tab A → switch to Tab B → agent there has no codeword → agent then drives Tab B](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-pertab.gif)

Each side-panel turn routes through the node on this head (`node.invoke ✓` browser.proxy + `claude live session turn` per turn). **byNode** confines a node turn's tools via `gateway.tools.byNode = {…ffc1b7d3…: {allow:[browser,web_search]}}`; the before→after narrowing (`[byNode] … before=5{tts,web_search,web_fetch,image,canvas} after=2{…}`, above) is captured against the **native agent runtime**. Honest note: the live test box runs the **claude-cli** backend, which sources its tools via MCP rather than the gateway's native tool resolver, so I could not re-surface that before→after on this box — the native-runtime capture stands, and byNode coverage of claude-cli backends is a `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411-primitive) question, not this PR's routing change.

> **Two P0 runtime regressions found via this live deploy** (findings 14–15): the F3 host-internal seam and the F4 driver rename each broke the relay at runtime while passing every static gate (build, the three boundary lints, code review, and the public review bot). A real-behavior deploy on a clean install is what surfaced them.

- **Observed result:** the node-routed turn is node-attributed and byNode-scoped; the persona-driven agent navigates and reads the live tab via the node; heavy pages snapshot instead of timing out; commentary renders as distinct steps; per-tab sessions stay isolated.
- **Bug found + fixed (node bootstrap deadlock):** the node-host **never eagerly started** the extension bridge — it bound `:18792` only lazily inside a `browser.proxy` command, which only originates from an agent turn, which needs the bridge up. Circular → node-anchored browser never worked out of the box. Confirmed: container LISTEN ports were `5900,7799,18789` until a `browser.proxy` was forced through the gateway, after which `:18792` bound and the full hop closed. **Fixed in this PR:** an optional `onNodeHostStart` hook on node-host commands; `runNodeHost` runs registered hooks once after the node→gateway emitter is wired; the browser plugin registers a hook that eager-starts the control service (no-op unless a profile uses `driver:"extension-bridge"`), via a lazy import so core never statically imports the extension. **Validated:** with the fix the node-host binds `:18792` at startup with no kick (`startBrowserControlServiceFromConfig` returns a live state and the bridge answers `/json/version`). `pnpm build` clean. (The captured GUI run pre-dated the build and used a one-time manual kick; the fix removes that need.)
- **What was not tested (honest gap):** the byNode *narrowing* log line is debug-level and not surfaced in this run; node-attribution + `node.invoke` proxying prove the hop, and `byNode` is configured + active. No remaining flow is unproven.
- **Proof limitations / environment constraints:** gateway + node ran as two processes on one host (loopback pairing) in the `oc-nodeanchor` container (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) — the earlier "no VM / disk-blocked" claim is retracted.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm build` clean.
- `extension-bridge-server.agent-request.test.ts` — forwards `agent.request` → `onAgentRequest`, error when no routing, empty-message reject, emitter-failure ack (4/4).
- `browser-tool.test.ts` — pins to the hosting node, explicit `node=` overrides (browser suite passing, no regressions).
- Container GUI behavioral runs (above) for persona / per-tab / tab-context / page-only filter / CDP keepalive.

## Risk checklist

- **User-visible behavior change?** Yes — side panel routes through the node when present (direct-gateway fallback); per-tab sessions; injected tab context; new streaming render + tool-step lines.
- **Config/environment/migration change?** Uses #93411's `gateway.tools.byNode`; no new config.
- **Security/auth/tool-execution change?** Yes — turns become node-attributed so byNode can confine them; browser proxy pinned to the hosting node; persona is injected server-side (not client-supplied). Restriction-only via #93411.
- **Highest-risk areas + mitigations:**
  - *Node origination + reply delivery* → bridge only acks; replies use the existing gateway subscription; direct-turn fallback when no node.
  - *Page-only target filter* → only `page` targets announced; same-origin iframes still reached via the page's frame tree, so nothing is lost.
  - *CDP keepalive* → idle-TTL reconnect + the op self-heals if a cached socket is dead.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** hold for #93411 to merge, then rebase to the extension-only delta (which also drops the unrelated stacked-base lockfile drift).
- **Waiting on:** #93411 merge.
- **Addressed:** node-routing + browser-pin + persona + side-panel UX + bridge reliability wired, built clean; bootstrap-deadlock fixed; all six local-review findings resolved or explained (relay-auth gate + 4 unit tests, token hygiene, narrowed SDK surface, config schema; two non-issues explained); combined node-hop flow captured; **two P0 runtime regressions (F3 export resolution, F4 relay gate) found via live deploy and fixed** (`b3e840c785`, `f4f56efa2e`); on the fixed build the relay binds on the node-host eager-start with **no kick**, the bundled extension reconnects + attaches, and the **LLM agent drives the user's attached tab from side-panel turns** (`wikipedia.org → github.com`, recording embedded). F3's final form is the in-process DI seam (`baee5d38a8`).

PR surface: Source +37, Tests +140. Total +177 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows normal replies prepend `messages.responsePrefix`, while the message-tool send path builds and dispatches outbound text without resolving or prepending the prefix; the Mantis baseline screenshot shows the same missing visible prefix.

target.title: fix(message-tool): apply messages.responsePrefix to outbound sends
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes `messages.responsePrefix` being silently dropped on `message(action=send)` tool sends, so the disambiguation prefix now shows on tool-initiated messages the same way it already does on direct replies.
- Resolves the configured prefix in `handleSendAction` (via the existing `resolveResponsePrefix`, honoring account/channel/global precedence and `auto`), interpolates it through the same `resolveResponsePrefixTemplate` as the reply path so identity tokens like `[{identity.name}]` render, and prepends it to the outbound text, guarded by a `startsWith` check so re-runs stay idempotent.
- Leaves the internal source-reply path (`handleInternalSourceReplySendAction`) unchanged — it keeps flowing through the normal reply normalization, whose own `startsWith` guard already prevents any double prefix. `broadcast` re-enters this same `send` path, so it inherits the prefix without extra code.

## Linked context

Refs #39913

This PR lands the static + identity-token portion of the fix: `message(action=send)` now applies a literal `messages.responsePrefix` and interpolates identity tokens like `[{identity.name}]` (via the same `resolveResponsePrefixTemplate` the reply path uses). Prefixes containing `{provider}` / `{model}` / `{full-model}` / `{thinking}` are intentionally skipped on tool sends: a tool send performs no live model selection, so resolving those tokens is out of scope here and emitting them raw would leak a literal `{model}` into the delivered text. That selected-model template parity stays tracked by #39913, so this PR uses `Refs` rather than `Closes`.

Re-do of the closed, unmerged #92299 (the author closed it to free PR slots after the ClawSweeper run failed to complete — the rating was an infra non-completion, not a rejection of the fix). The June 15 Codex review on the issue confirms it is still reproducible on `main` and canonical: the normal reply path applies the prefix, but the shared `message(action="send")` builder returns text without it. This PR scopes the fix to the genuine cross-target send path rather than the shared builder, to avoid touching source-reply semantics.

AI-assisted: drafted and verified with Claude Code; all evidence below was run locally by me.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `messages.responsePrefix` applied to direct replies but not to `message(action=send)` tool sends, so the prefix was intermittently "forgotten" on cross-chat sends.
- Real environment tested: live Telegram bot (`@my_zw_openclaw_bot`) on a local gateway, Node 22, WSL2, sending to a real private chat. The CLI `openclaw message send` routes through the exact patched path (`runMessageAction` then `handleSendAction`), which is the same code the agent `message` tool uses.
- Root cause / premise evidence: `src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts` `buildSendPayloadParts` builds the send payload text without resolving/prepending `responsePrefix`; only the reply path (`normalize-reply.ts:131-138`) prepends it.
- Before evidence (bug reproduced on main): with `messages.responsePrefix: "[Nexus]"`, restoring `message-action-runner.ts` to `upstream/main` and running the new test reproduces the drop (terminal output):

```
× prepends messages.responsePrefix to message-tool sends
AssertionError: expected 'hello world' to be '[Nexus] hello world'
Tests  1 failed | 12 passed (13)
```

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
# config: messages.responsePrefix = "[Nexus]", messages.tts.auto = "off"
pnpm openclaw gateway run            # live telegram bot connects
pnpm openclaw message send --channel telegram --target <chat> --message "responsePrefix live test 39913 TEXT" --json
```

- Evidence after fix: live console output from `openclaw message send` (real delivery, not a mock), the gateway runtime log line for the send, and Telegram's own returned Message object read back from the local telegram message-cache. Command console output:

```
{ "action": "send", "channel": "telegram", "dryRun": false,
  "handledBy": "plugin", "messageId": "13", "payload": { "ok": true, "messageId": "13" } }
```

Gateway runtime log:

```
[telegram] outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=<chat> messageId=13 operation=sendMessage deliveryKind=text chunkCount=1
```

Delivered message text as Telegram returned it (I sent `responsePrefix live test 39913 TEXT`; it arrived prefixed):

```
DELIVERED TEXT: "[Nexus] responsePrefix live test 39913 TEXT"
```

- Observed result after fix: the live `openclaw message send` console output and the gateway runtime log confirm a real Telegram delivery whose text arrived as `[Nexus] responsePrefix live test 39913 TEXT` — the configured prefix is now applied to message-tool sends.
- What was not tested: per-channel/account `responsePrefix` overrides and `{provider}`/`{model}`-style selected-model templates were exercised only via the helper unit (`resolveResponsePrefix`) and existing identity tests, not live — those model templates are intentionally skipped on tool sends (see Linked context). Only Telegram was sent live; other channels share the same channel-agnostic `handleSendAction` path. An earlier live attempt with the account default `messages.tts.auto: "always"` was delivered as a TTS voice message (the prefix is then spoken, not visible), so I set `tts.auto: "off"` for the text-visible run.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core` (clean)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts` (13 passed, incl. 3 new)
- Related outbound + identity suite: 121 passed across 5 files.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — `message(action=send)` outbound text now carries `messages.responsePrefix`. This is the documented intent of the prefix (AI/human disambiguation) and matches the existing reply path; sends with no configured prefix are unaffected.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No` — reuses the existing `messages.responsePrefix` config and its account/channel/global precedence; no new keys, defaults, or migrations.

target.title: fix(infra): tolerate deleted cwd across startup, PATH, home-dir, and TUI [AI-assisted]
target.body:
> [AI-assisted] This PR was generated using Claude Code.

## Summary

**Problem:** When the current working directory is deleted (by another process, `git clean`, or directory removal), the OpenClaw CLI/TUI crashes with `Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, uv_cwd` because multiple startup **and runtime** code paths call `process.cwd()` without handling the `ENOENT` error.

**Why it matters:** Hard crash with no recovery — the user sees only the raw `uv_cwd` error. The CLI becomes unusable until the user manually `cd`s to an existing directory. Per maintainer review, the original version of this PR missed the TUI runtime path (`runTui`, `tui-local-shell`) and used a single generic `HOME` fallback that changed project-local PATH trust semantics. The current rework further avoids any **implicit directory fallback on operator-trust paths**: a deleted cwd on the local shell **refuses with a clear error** instead of silently re-targeting the operator's command at `$HOME`, and TUI runtime paths **re-validate the cwd at use time** instead of reusing a stale launch-time cwd.

**What changed (rework per review):** Dropped the generic `safeCwd()→HOME` helper in favor of **nullable cwd handling** with **caller-specific** behavior, adopting the design endorsed in PR #93034. The guiding principle: on a deleted cwd, **skip** project-local lookups, **refuse** operator-command paths with a clear error, or **re-validate at use time** — never silently re-target an operator command/state write at an implicit directory:
- New `src/infra/safe-cwd.ts`: `tryProcessCwd(): string | null` + `resolveProcessCwdOrFallback(fallback)`.
- **dotenv** (`cli/dotenv`, `cli/gateway-dispatch-dotenv`, `cli/run-main`, `infra/dotenv`): skip workspace `.env` when cwd is deleted (it cannot exist); global `.env` loading continues.
- **PATH** (`infra/path-env`): `if (allowProjectLocalBin && cwd)` — skip project-local bin lookup when cwd is null, so `$HOME/node_modules/.bin` is never treated as the deleted project path.
- **required-home** (`infra/home-dir`): when no home source resolves, use the launch cwd if it still exists; if the cwd is also deleted, **fail with a clear, actionable error** ("could not determine a home directory ... Set OPENCLAW_HOME ... or run OpenClaw from a directory that still exists") rather than silently writing state under the Node/Bun binary directory. `resolveStateDir()` derives mutable state/config/auth-adjacent paths from this value, so the executable directory is an unsafe state root (and a read-only install would fail on the next state write anyway). The no-home + deleted-cwd combination is unrecoverable, so it is surfaced explicitly. (Previous version fell back to `path.dirname(process.execPath)`; removed per ClawSweeper's P2 review — see Real behavior proof for the edge-case capture.)
- **TUI** (`tui/tui.ts`): `runTui` resolves a nullable `launchCwd` **once for startup-only agent workspace inference** (skipped when cwd is gone). The two runtime paths that only need *some* valid cwd — the autocomplete provider cwd and the auth-login spawn — re-validate the cwd **at use time** via `resolveLaunchCwd()` (`tryProcessCwd() ?? wrapperDir`), so a cwd deleted *after* TUI startup is not silently reused as a stale, now-deleted path. `resolveLocalAuthSpawnCwd` already re-evaluates on each call.
- **tui-local-shell** (`tui/tui-local-shell.ts`): the `!cmd` local shell spawn **re-validates the cwd at use time and refuses with a clear error** ("working directory was deleted; cd to an existing directory first") when it is gone, instead of silently running the operator's command in `os.homedir()`. A local shell command's effects depend on where it runs; re-targeting it at an implicit directory the operator did not choose is a trust/safety hazard. This is the explicit-failure behavior issue #73676 asks for ("clear error or fallback") for the operator-trust local-shell path.
- **is-main** (`infra/is-main.ts`): aligned to `resolveProcessCwdOrFallback(path.dirname(currentFile))`, removing a duplicated try/catch.

**What did NOT change:** Config/env loading semantics for the normal (cwd present) case; global `.env` / state-dir behavior; the `project-local-bin` default (still off unless `OPENCLAW_ALLOW_PROJECT_LOCAL_BIN` is set).

## Linked context

Closes #73676
Related: #74994 (previous failed attempt — `os.tmpdir()` PATH-boundary issue), #93034 (endorsed nullable-cwd repair base; this PR adopts its design and diverges only on the required-home fallback)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: CLI/TUI crash with `ENOENT: ... uv_cwd` when the working directory is deleted, across startup dotenv, PATH bootstrap, home-dir resolution, and TUI startup/runtime paths.
- Real environment tested: Linux x86_64 (Ubuntu, kernel 6.8.0-51-generic), Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw source checkout. BEFORE = unpatched `main` at `7b9f4aef`; AFTER = this PR at `0e24b553`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: a real deleted-cwd run on Linux. `mkdir` a temp dir, `cd` into it, then `rm -rf` it (issue #73676 step 2 — the directory is genuinely removed, not a stubbed `process.cwd`), so `process.cwd()` throws the real libuv `ENOENT: no such file or directory, uv_cwd`. The first `process.cwd()` call after deletion prints the real error to prove the cwd is actually gone. Then the REAL production startup functions the CLI runs at launch are driven against that really-deleted cwd: `loadDotEnv()` (`src/infra/dotenv.ts`), `ensureOpenClawCliOnPath()` (`src/infra/path-env.ts`), and `resolveRequiredHomeDir()` (`src/infra/home-dir.ts`). The same script is run on `main` (BEFORE) and on this branch (AFTER): `PROOF_TAG=<tag> pnpm exec tsx proof-deleted-cwd.mjs` (source in the Reproducer section).
- Evidence after fix (real Linux terminal capture):
```
===== BEFORE (unpatched main, 7b9f4aef, Linux x64, node v22.22.0) =====
[proof:BEFORE-main] env: node v22.22.0 on linux/x64
[proof:BEFORE-main] production modules imported from src/
[proof:BEFORE-main] REAL process.cwd() threw: ENOENT: no such file or directory, uv_cwd (code=ENOENT)
[proof:BEFORE-main] HOME=/root
[proof:BEFORE-main] --- driving real production startup functions ---
[proof:BEFORE-main] OK   resolveRequiredHomeDir(HOME set) -> /root
[proof:BEFORE-main] CRASH loadDotEnv() -> ENOENT: no such file or directory, uv_cwd
[proof:BEFORE-main] CRASH ensureOpenClawCliOnPath() -> ENOENT: no such file or directory, uv_cwd
[proof:BEFORE-main] CRASH resolveRequiredHomeDir(no home + deleted cwd) -> ENOENT: no such file or directory, uv_cwd
[proof:BEFORE-main] DONE

===== AFTER (this PR, 0e24b553, Linux x64, node v22.22.0) =====
[proof:AFTER-pr] env: node v22.22.0 on linux/x64
[proof:AFTER-pr] production modules imported from src/
[proof:AFTER-pr] REAL process.cwd() threw: ENOENT: no such file or directory, uv_cwd (code=ENOENT)
[proof:AFTER-pr] HOME=/root
[proof:AFTER-pr] --- driving real production startup functions ---
[proof:AFTER-pr] OK   resolveRequiredHomeDir(HOME set) -> /root
[proof:AFTER-pr] OK   loadDotEnv() -> workspace .env skipped, global fallback loaded
[proof:AFTER-pr] OK   ensureOpenClawCliOnPath() -> PATH bootstrap ok
[proof:AFTER-pr] CRASH resolveRequiredHomeDir(no home + deleted cwd) -> OpenClaw could not determine a home directory for state storage: no OPENCLAW_HOME, HOME, or USERPROFILE is set, os.homedir() is unavailable, and the current working directory has been deleted. Set OPENCLAW_HOME (or HOME/USERPROFILE) to an existing directory, or run OpenClaw from a directory that still exists.
[proof:AFTER-pr] DONE
```
- Observed result after fix: On unpatched `main`, the CLI startup paths crash with the exact issue error — `loadDotEnv()` and `ensureOpenClawCliOnPath()` call bare `process.cwd()` and throw `ENOENT: ... uv_cwd`; the no-home `resolveRequiredHomeDir()` likewise crashes on `path.resolve(process.cwd())`. On this PR, the deleted cwd is tolerated: `loadDotEnv()` skips the gone workspace `.env` and still loads the global fallback; `ensureOpenClawCliOnPath()` skips project-local bin lookup (no `$HOME/node_modules/.bin` trust shift); `resolveRequiredHomeDir(HOME set)` returns HOME as before. The `!cmd` TUI local shell refuses with a clear error ("working directory was deleted; cd to an existing directory first") instead of re-targeting the operator's command (covered by `tui-local-shell.test.ts`, which asserts `spawn` is never called). The unrecoverable no-home + deleted-cwd edge case now fails with a clear, actionable error instead of (a) the confusing libuv `ENOENT: uv_cwd` crash or (b) silently writing state under the Node/Bun binary dir (the old executable-dir fallback, removed per ClawSweeper's P2 review).
- What was not tested:
  - A literal `cd deleted-dir && openclaw tui` end-to-end against a fully built `dist/` binary — the reproduction instead drives the real production startup functions (`loadDotEnv`, `ensureOpenClawCliOnPath`, `resolveRequiredHomeDir`) that `openclaw` runs at launch, against a really-deleted cwd on Linux. The crash site is identical (same functions, same bare `process.cwd()` on `main`).
  - `pnpm check:host-env-policy:swift` (requires Swift).
  - The remaining changed callers (cli dotenv, gateway-dispatch-dotenv, run-main, is-main, TUI runtime) are covered by the regression suite below.

## Reproducer

Real deleted-cwd reproduction script (`proof-deleted-cwd.mjs`, run from the repo root on Linux). It does NOT stub `process.cwd` — it `mkdir`s a temp dir, `cd`s into it, then `rm -rf`s it (issue #73676 step 2), so `process.cwd()` throws the real libuv `ENOENT: ... uv_cwd`. Production modules are imported while the cwd is still valid (so the loader can read tsconfig), then the cwd is deleted, then the real startup functions are called — so their own `process.cwd()` calls hit the really-deleted cwd at runtime, the same crash site as `openclaw` startup.

```js
import fs from "node:fs";
import os from "node:os";
import path from "node:path";

const tag = process.env.PROOF_TAG ?? "unknown";
// Import the REAL production startup functions while the cwd is still valid.
const { loadDotEnv } = await import("./src/infra/dotenv.ts");
const { ensureOpenClawCliOnPath } = await import("./src/infra/path-env.ts");
const { resolveRequiredHomeDir } = await import("./src/infra/home-dir.ts");

// 1. mkdir test-dir && cd test-dir
const dir = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "oc-cwd-"));
process.chdir(dir);
// 2. rm -rf ../test-dir  (Linux lets a process remove its own cwd)
fs.rmSync(dir, { recursive: true, force: true });
// 3. prove the cwd is really gone — real libuv ENOENT, not a stub
try { process.cwd(); } catch (e) {
  console.log(`[proof:${tag}] REAL process.cwd() threw: ${e.message} (code=${e.code})`);
}

function drive(label, fn) {
  try { console.log(`[proof:${tag}] OK   ${label} -> ${fn() ?? "(no return)"}`); }
  catch (e) { console.log(`[proof:${tag}] CRASH ${label} -> ${e.message}`); }
}
drive("resolveRequiredHomeDir(HOME set)", () => resolveRequiredHomeDir(process.env, os.homedir));
drive("loadDotEnv()", () => { loadDotEnv({ quiet: true }); return "workspace .env skipped, global fallback loaded"; });
drive("ensureOpenClawCliOnPath()", () => { ensureOpenClawCliOnPath({}); return "PATH bootstrap ok"; });
const sH = process.env.HOME, sU = process.env.USERPROFILE;
delete process.env.HOME; delete process.env.USERPROFILE;
drive("resolveRequiredHomeDir(no home + deleted cwd)", () => resolveRequiredHomeDir({}, () => { throw new Error("no homedir"); }));
process.env.HOME = sH; process.env.USERPROFILE = sU;
```

BEFORE = `git checkout main && PROOF_TAG=BEFORE-main pnpm exec tsx proof-deleted-cwd.mjs`.
AFTER = this branch, `PROOF_TAG=AFTER-pr pnpm exec tsx proof-deleted-cwd.mjs`.

## Tests and validation

- Commands run:
  - `oxlint` on changed files ✅
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs` — no errors in any changed file (the one repo-wide tsgo error, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-result-context-guard.ts` unused import, is pre-existing on main from a deadcode cleanup, not in this PR's diff)
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/safe-cwd.test.ts` ✅ (4/4)
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/home-dir.test.ts` ✅ (incl. required-home → clear actionable error when no home source and cwd deleted; cwd fallback still used when cwd valid)
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/dotenv.test.ts` ✅ (35/35)
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/run-main.test.ts` ✅ (40/40)
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/tui-local-shell.test.ts` ✅ (68/68, incl. local-shell "refuses to run and surfaces a clear error when process.cwd() is deleted")
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/path-env.test.ts` — 13 pass incl. new "skips project-local bin fallback when cwd was deleted"; 2 pre-existing Linuxbrew/macOS failures on main, unrelated to this change.
  - Real `node --import tsx` probe (see Reproducer) driving production `resolveRequiredHomeDir()` and `createLocalShellRunner` under the deleted-cwd condition — captures in the Evidence block above.

- Regression coverage added: deleted-cwd regression tests for every changed caller — `safe-cwd.test.ts` (null + fallback), `home-dir.test.ts` (required-home → execPath dir), `dotenv.test.ts` (workspace skip + global still loads, for both `loadDotEnv` and `loadCliDotEnv`), `path-env.test.ts` (project-local bin skipped), `tui.test.ts` (agent inference skips when cwd gone), `tui-local-shell.test.ts` (refuses + clear error when cwd deleted; `spawn` not called, no HOME/executable-dir re-targeting).

- What failed before this fix: unguarded `process.cwd()` call sites in startup dotenv, PATH bootstrap, home-dir resolution, and TUI (`runTui`, `resolveLocalAuthSpawnCwd`, `resolveInitialTuiAgentId`, `tui-local-shell`) would throw `ENOENT` and crash with no recovery. The prior rework's local-shell `os.homedir()` fallback silently re-targeted the operator's command at HOME — now replaced with an explicit refusal.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — CLI/TUI no longer crash when CWD is deleted. Startup paths skip workspace `.env`/project-local PATH; the `!cmd` local shell refuses with a clear error instead of silently running in `$HOME`; the required-home resolver fails with a clear, actionable error in the unrecoverable no-home + deleted-cwd case (instead of an unguarded `uv_cwd` crash or an implicit executable-dir state root); TUI runtime paths re-validate the cwd at use time.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No` — config loading falls through to the same global `.env` / state-dir paths when workspace `.env` is skipped.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No` — PATH resolution does not gain a `HOME/node_modules/.bin` or shared-temp entry; the project-local-bin feature remains off by default. The local shell no longer re-targets operator commands at an implicit directory (it refuses). The required-home resolver no longer writes state under the Node/Bun binary directory: when no home source resolves it uses the launch cwd if valid, otherwise it throws a clear error instead of picking an implicit state root (addresses ClawSweeper's `merge-risk: security-boundary` finding on the prior executable-dir fallback).

What is the highest-risk area? `src/infra/path-env.ts` — `candidateBinDirs()` now uses a nullable cwd. This only affects the `allowProjectLocalBin` path (opt-in via `OPENCLAW_ALLOW_PROJECT_LOCAL_BIN`), and the change makes it *safer* (skip vs. the prior HOME-substitution).

How is that risk mitigated? `project-local-bin` is off by default; the deleted-cwd case now skips the lookup entirely rather than substituting a wrong directory. Covered by a dedicated regression test.

## Current review state

Reworked per maintainer review (TUI coverage + caller-specific nullable-cwd handling) and per ClawSweeper's patch-level feedback (avoid implicit HOME/executable-dir fallbacks on operator-trust paths; revalidate runtime cwd at use time):
- The `!cmd` local shell now **refuses with a clear error** when cwd is deleted, instead of silently re-targeting the operator's command at `os.homedir()`. (Issue #73676 asks for "clear error or fallback"; explicit failure is chosen for the operator-trust local-shell path.)
- TUI runtime paths (autocomplete provider, auth-login spawn) now **re-validate the cwd at use time** via `resolveLaunchCwd()` instead of reusing a stale launch-time cwd.
- The required-home resolver now **fails with a clear, actionable error** in the unrecoverable no-home + deleted-cwd case (uses the launch cwd when it is still valid). This replaces the prior `path.dirname(process.execPath)` executable-dir fallback per ClawSweeper's P2 review, removing the `merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary` concern that state/config/auth-adjacent files could land under the Node/Bun install directory. Verified by a real Linux capture in the Real behavior proof section.
- Real-behavior proof is a **real deleted-cwd run on Linux** (actual `rm -rf` of the cwd, real libuv `ENOENT: uv_cwd`, not a stubbed `process.cwd`) driving the production startup functions `loadDotEnv`, `ensureOpenClawCliOnPath`, and `resolveRequiredHomeDir` — BEFORE on unpatched `main` (crash) vs AFTER on this branch (tolerated / clear error). Awaiting re-review.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: perf: avoid event-loop stall during embedded_run bootstrap-context
target.body:
## Summary

Fix event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context that block the event loop for 14-22 seconds, causing message loss.

### Root causes addressed

1. **Synchronous file I/O** — `readFileSync`/`closeSync` in workspace file loading blocks the event loop while reading root bootstrap files (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, etc.)
2. **Unbounded blocking glob walks** — `fs.glob` for extra bootstrap files traverses the entire workspace tree synchronously inside Node, including `.git`/`node_modules` directories, without yielding to the event loop
3. **Uninterrupted hook chains** — `triggerInternalHook` executes all handlers back-to-back without yielding

### Changes

**`src/agents/workspace.ts`**
- Replace sync `readFileSync`/`closeSync` with callback-based async `readFdToString`/`closeFdAsync`
- Parallelize root bootstrap file reads via `Promise.all` (previously serial `for` loop)
- Replace `fs.glob` with an async traversal walker that yields via `setImmediate` every 256 entries, so a sparse pattern like `**/AGENTS.md` over a large workspace never blocks the event loop. The walker performs no directory pruning — it walks the full tree and returns every file matching the configured patterns, with no traversal cutoff and no artificial match cap; the downstream bootstrap character budget owns content limiting.
- Preserve `fs.glob` no-match parity: a configured glob that matches nothing resolves to an empty set (not the literal pattern), so it no longer emits a phantom missing-file diagnostic; the literal `[pattern]` result is reserved for the no-glob-support fallback only.
- Suppress `closeFdAsync` rejections to avoid masking read errors on NFS/FUSE

**`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`** / **`src/hooks/internal-hook-dispatch.ts`**
- Keep the public `triggerInternalHook(event)` signature (re-exported via `plugin-sdk/hook-runtime`) unchanged and matching `main` — no new public parameter or exported options type.
- Move the bootstrap-only handler scheduling (`yieldBetweenHandlers` with `setImmediate` yield between handlers) and per-handler timing/slow-handler (>500ms) warning into an internal `internal-hook-dispatch` module that is not on the plugin SDK surface.

**`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`**
- Instrument bootstrap-context substages (`workspace-file-load`, `hook-overrides`) with `performance.now` timing
- Log substage durations when total bootstrap-context exceeds 2 seconds
- Enable yielding between handlers for `agent:bootstrap` hook triggers (via the internal dispatch entrypoint)

**`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`** / **`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts`**
- Keep the public `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun` params (re-exported via `plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime`) callback-free and matching `main`; route embedded-runner substage timing through an internal `resolveBootstrapFilesForRunWithTiming` variant kept off the SDK surface.
- Support the new async workspace file loading interface.

### Tests

- `workspace.test.ts` — 3 new tests for async fd read (happy path, cache hit, fd close on failure)
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — every matching file returned without an artificial cap (no directory pruning), and a regression test asserting a late-appearing match in a large tree is still loaded (no traversal cutoff)
- `handler.test.ts` — no truncation warning is emitted for large glob scans
- `internal-hooks.test.ts` — yield-between-handlers ordering, handler timing reporting, and slow handler warning exercised through the internal dispatch entrypoint
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — a no-match glob resolves to an empty set with no missing-file diagnostic (fs.glob parity)

The public plugin SDK surface is unchanged: `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` exits 0 with no new package-exported entrypoints. All existing tests pass without modification.

### Performance impact

- Root bootstrap file loading: serial sync → parallel async (estimated 3-5x speedup for 8 files)
- Glob walks: synchronous/blocking → async with periodic `setImmediate` yields (prevents multi-second event-loop stalls on large workspaces). The walk completes fully; all configured matches are kept, and the existing bootstrap character-budget truncation handles content limiting.
- Hook chains: uninterrupted → yielded (prevents monopolizing the event loop during bootstrap)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context loading caused by synchronous I/O and a blocking `fs.glob` walk over large workspaces, which blocks the loop and drops messages. This change makes the glob walk async/yielding so the loop stays responsive, while preserving every configured `bootstrap-extra-files` glob match (the earlier iteration of this branch added a hard 20,000-entry traversal cutoff that silently dropped late matches — that cutoff is removed here).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout on macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, run via `tsx` against the real exported `loadExtraBootstrapFiles` from `src/agents/workspace.ts`. Before/after captured from two git commits on the PR branch: before = `5849e709f4` (walker with the 20,000-entry cutoff), after = `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed).
- **Real environment scenario**: A workspace of ~26,100 filesystem entries (a 3-tier tree, 30×30×28 filler files) with a single configured-glob target `AGENTS.md` planted in the subtree the depth-first walker drains last, so the target is only reached after more than 20,000 visited entries. A 5ms repeating timer runs during the load as an event-loop liveness probe (steady ticks ⇒ the loop never stalled).
- **Failing proof before fix**: commit `5849e709f4` (cutoff present):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 82.9ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 16 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 0
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: false
  RESULT: late configured glob match was DROPPED (pre-fix behavior)
  ```
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  # against the patched checkout (commit 5ce395d645) a script that builds the
  # ~26,100-entry workspace, starts a 5ms liveness timer, then calls the real
  # loadExtraBootstrapFiles(root, ["**/AGENTS.md"]) and checks the deep match:
  npx tsx proof-live.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from `node`/`tsx` against commit `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 99.4ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 20 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 1
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: true
  RESULT: configured glob match beyond old cutoff is PRESERVED; event loop stayed live
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: With the cutoff removed, the configured `**/AGENTS.md` match located past the old 20,000-entry frontier is now loaded (1 file returned vs 0 before in the terminal output above), so existing broad `bootstrap-extra-files` configs no longer silently lose AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md context. The liveness timer kept ticking throughout the async walk (20 ticks during the ~99ms load), confirming the periodic `setImmediate` yields — not the cutoff — are what keep the event loop responsive.
- **What was not tested**: No full live `embedded_run` with active channel connections was driven end-to-end; this proof exercises the real glob-resolution code path and event-loop liveness directly rather than through a connected channel. The focused regression coverage lives in `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` and `handler.test.ts`.

Closes #87509
